_meta.note: Source-of-truth keys for Pulse. Translate VALUES only; never change keys. {placeholders} stay as-is.

accessoriesSettings.modemAutoSetupEnable: Modem zal automatisch instellen bij starten app / achtergrond service
appHome.updateVersionReady: Versie {version} is klaar om te installeren
automationsSettings.deleteCannotUndo: Deze actie kan niet ongedaan worden.
automationsSettings.emptyPresets: No climate presets. Tap + to create one.
automationsSettings.emptySchedules: No preconditioning schedules. Tap + to add one.
automationsSettings.emptyWakeUps: No scheduled wake ups. Tap + to add one.
automationsSettings.paywallBody: Scheduled wake-ups and preconditioning need an active Pulse subscription. Your saved schedules are kept and will resume the moment your subscription is renewed.
bluetoothSettings.autoConnectSubtitleAct: When the car starts, Pulse will reconnect to {name} automatically. If it's already connected, Pulse drops the link first and re-handshakes for a fresh audio path.
bluetoothSettings.autoConnectSubtitleIna: Select a Fisker ICC below to enable this option.
bluetoothSettings.connectFailed: Could not request connect — check Bluetooth is on
bluetoothSettings.emptyBtOffBody: Turn on Bluetooth in the system settings to see paired devices.
bluetoothSettings.emptyNoPairedBody: Pair the Fisker Ocean ICC through the system Bluetooth settings first — Pulse only lists already-paired devices.
bluetoothSettings.hintBluetoothOff: Bluetooth is off. Turn it on in the system Bluetooth settings to pair and select your Fisker Ocean ICC.
bluetoothSettings.hintPickIcc: Pick the Fisker ICC from the bonded list below (usually "FiskerOcean"). Pulse will use it for the auto-connect on ignition.
bluetoothSettings.iccSet: Set "{name}" as the Fisker Ocean ICC
canTestSettings.headerDescription: Developer tools for injecting CAN frames. Use with care — frames are sent on IBUS1 and the car will react as if the steering wheel button was pressed.
canTestSettings.rightWestFailed: Failed to inject Right West press
canTestSettings.steeringWheelDescription: Tapping below sends a single short press of the chosen button. Pulse waits up to 200 ms for a fresh frame from the real MFSS so the alive counter and E2E P01 checksum align with the live bus, then injects the press immediately after.
canTestSettings.title: CAN Bus Test
canTestSettings.waitingForFrame: Waiting for next MFS frame, then injecting…
carHealthSettings.alreadyHaveDistanceSub: Pulse will subtract from current odometer.
carHealthSettings.brandNewSubtitle: Pulse will count from current odometer.
carHealthSettings.noSolarDataForDay: No solar data for this day
carHealthSettings.recordRotationContent: We'll mark the rotation at the current odometer ({v}). The countdown to your next rotation will reset.
carHealthSettings.rotationIntervalHelper: Default 7,500 mi (≈ 12,070 km) — adjust to taste
carHealthSettings.tireNewOrUsedQuestion: Are these tires brand new, or do they already have distance on them?
carHealthSettings.tireSetupDescription: Pulse can remind you when to rotate your tires based on real driven distance from the odometer. Tell us how many miles are already on the current set, and how often you want to rotate.
carHealthSettings.trackingExistingTires: Tracking existing tires ({v} already on them)
carHealthSettings.trackingNewTires: Tracking new tires from current odometer
climateCard.title: Climate
cloudSettings.clickLinkInEmail: Click the link in the email, then the app will detect the confirmation automatically.
cloudSettings.confirmationLinkSent: A confirmation link has been sent to your email.
cloudSettings.emailHelperText: This email will be used to regain access to your Pulse device if something happens.
cloudSettings.giveNameToYourCar: Give a name to your car!
cloudSettings.noAccountsLinked: No accounts linked to this device.
cloudSettings.pinSentToCurrentOwner: A PIN was sent to the current owner
cloudSettings.pinSentToOwner: A PIN was sent to the current owner's email. Ask them for the PIN to claim this device.
cloudSettings.scanQrCode:
Scan this QR code with the OceanLink Pro app
to link this device to your dashboard account.
cloudSettings.title: Cloud Settings
diagnosticsPage.mcpIssueNoInterface: kernel logs mention an MCP issue; see the dmesg lines below.
diagnosticsPage.mcpIssueWithInterface: kernel logs mention an MCP issue and {interface} is down. Most likely the MCP2515 on one of the SPI buses below failed to initialise at boot.
diagnosticsPage.reloadDescription: Unloads and reloads kernel module then brings IBUS1/IBUS2 back up. Use this if a bus is down or up but not receiving frames.
diagnosticsPage.reloadDialogContent: This unloads and reloads the mcp251x kernel module with the correct hardware filters, then brings IBUS1/IBUS2 back up. CAN reception drops for a few seconds while it reinitialises.
diagnosticsPage.servicesMustBeForeground: Each must be Running + Foreground (not just Bound).
diagnosticsPage.title: Diagnostics
displaySettings.blindSpotOnSubtitle: On — bars light up when ADAS reports a threat.
displaySettings.floatingButtonOnSubtitle: Visible across the system. Drag to move.
displaySettings.floatingResizeButtonDesc: Adds a small transparent button above every app. Tap it to switch the device window-manager size between full and compact. Drag the button to reposition it.
displaySettings.navBarOffSubtitle: Off — only the resize button is shown.
displaySettings.navBarOnSubtitle: Adds Back / Home / Recent below the resize button.
displaySettings.overlayPermissionBody: The floating resize button is drawn on top of other apps, which requires the "Display over other apps" permission. Open the system settings to grant it now?
displaySettings.trafficLightOnSubtitle: On — shows when ADAS detects a traffic light.
esimSettings.checkingCloudBody: This usually takes a couple of seconds.
esimSettings.checkingCloudTitle: Checking the cloud for FOA eSIM data…
esimSettings.connectToWifiBody: Pulse couldn’t reach the OceanLink Pulse cloud. Connect this Pulse to Wi-Fi (or wait until cellular data is up) and try again.
esimSettings.couldNotActivateAfterRetrie: Couldn’t activate the new profile after 3 tries. Open the Profiles list and tap Enable manually.
esimSettings.deleteProfileActiveWarning: This profile is currently active — Pulse will deactivate it first, then delete it.
esimSettings.foaApnSet: FOA APN set. The modem will use it on the next attach.
esimSettings.foaFoundButCouldNotActivate: Found the FOA profile on the eUICC but couldn’t activate it. In the modem page, just under this message, click read/refresh Profiles and tap Enable on the FOA profile.
esimSettings.foaProfileAlreadyActive: FOA profile already installed and active.
esimSettings.installProfileHint: Enter the SM-DP+ details from your carrier’s activation email or QR code. Confirmation code is optional and only needed if the carrier required one.
esimSettings.installProfileManuallyBody: Enter an SM-DP+ URL and matching ID from a carrier activation email or QR code.
esimSettings.modemConnectedFailHint: Plug the cellular modem into a USB port that is NOT the blue one and NOT the single USB on the side (that one is for CarPlay / Android Auto). Use one of the other USB ports on the Pulse.
esimSettings.modemConnectedRecoveringHin: Resetting modem (AT+CFUN=1,1) — usually 15–25 seconds. Please don’t pull the SIM or unplug the modem.
esimSettings.noFoaEsimLinkedBody: To get a FOA eSIM provisioned for this Pulse, sign in to the OceanLink Pulse dashboard, click on your Pulse / car, and link your FOA account.
esimSettings.noIccidReturnedCannotActiva: No ICCID returned by install — cannot activate.
esimSettings.noProfilesInstalled: No profiles installed on this eUICC.
esimSettings.notificationsStuck: Processed {processed} of {total}; {stuckCount} stuck (seq {seqs}). Check logcat for the per-seq error.
esimSettings.profilesNotReadYetBody: Tap “Read profiles” to list what’s installed on the eUICC. This wakes the modem and takes a few seconds.
esimSettings.programmingFoaApn: Programming FOA APN — this takes a few seconds…
esimSettings.simCme11: SIM is present but locked — PIN required (CME error 11).
esimSettings.simCme12: SIM is present but locked — PUK required (CME error 12).
esimSettings.simCme13: Modem reported a SIM failure (CME error 13).
esimSettings.simCme14: SIM is busy — try again in a moment (CME error 14).
esimSettings.simCme15: Wrong SIM card for this modem (CME error 15).
esimSettings.simNoCpinResponse: Modem didn’t return a CPIN response. This can happen for ~30 seconds after a modem reset while the SIM driver comes back up — wait a moment and tap “Check again”.
esimSettings.simPresentFailHint:
{reason}

Insert the SIM into the tray. If it’s already inserted, double-check the orientation, or gently wiggle it to reseat the contacts.
esimSettings.simRecoveringHintModemReset: Modem is resetting. The SIM driver will come back up shortly after.
esimSettings.simRecoveringHintRecovery: Power-cycling the radio to recover the SIM (CFUN=0 → CFUN=1) — about 15 seconds. Don’t pull the SIM during this step.
esimSettings.simRecoveringHintWaiting: Waiting for the SIM driver to come back up. This can take 30–60 seconds after a modem reset or fresh boot.
esimSettings.smdpAndMatchingIdRequired: SM-DP+ URL and Matching ID are required.
hdmiInputPage.hdmiClosedNotInPark: HDMI Input closed — vehicle is no longer in Park.
headerCard.raceModeUnlockedContent:
License granted. The pit crew salutes you. 🏎️

Race Mode is now in your garage — Settings → Pulse → Race Mode. Go put down a {target} time and don't embarrass us.
language.contributeBody: Pulse is translated by its community. Add or improve a language on GitHub — your changes reach everyone without an app update.
language.downloadFailed: Couldn't download that language. Check your connection and try again.
language.offlineNote: Showing the cached list. Connect to the internet to refresh.
language.subtitle: Choose your language. Translations are contributed by the community.
language.title: Language
mediaCard.accessDescription:
Allow Pulse to read the now-playing
session to show controls here.
mediaCard.title: Media
navigationCard.title: Navigation
onboardingWizard.allSetMessage: You're all set! Tap Finish to start using Pulse.
onboardingWizard.autoConnectPkcSubtitle: Connects automatically when the app starts
onboardingWizard.bleKeyNotRetrieved: Could not retrieve BLE key. Is the car in Ready mode?
onboardingWizard.bleKeyReadyModeWarning: Make sure the car is in Ready mode before reading the BLE key.
onboardingWizard.bleKeySubtitle: Read the BLE key from your vehicle to enable phone-as-key features like remote lock/unlock.
onboardingWizard.installationTestSubtitl: Let's verify that Pulse is properly connected to your vehicle's CAN buses.
onboardingWizard.pleaseEnterName: Please enter first and last name
onboardingWizard.preferencesSubtitle: Set your display preferences. You can change these later in Settings.
onboardingWizard.profileSubtitle: Set up your first profile for personalized vehicle features.
onboardingWizard.readyModeInstructions:
Put your car in Ready mode:
1. Press the brake pedal
2. Wait for the car to turn on
3. The dashboard should light up
onboardingWizard.setupLaterHint: You can also set this up later in Settings → Vehicle
profileManagementPage.addDeviceMenuSubti: Select the kind of device to add
profileManagementPage.atLeastOneProfileR: At least one profile must remain
profileManagementPage.generateQrDescript: Generate a provisioning QR for this profile. The QR will contain the generated DEVICE_ID and BLE key (16 bytes). Scan this QR from the phone/smartwatch to provision.
profileManagementPage.holdKeyCardOnReade: Hold the key card on the car's interior NFC reader…
profileManagementPage.holdKeyFobOnReader: Hold the key fob on the car's interior NFC reader…
profileManagementPage.pleaseEnterFirstAn: Please enter first and last name
profileManagementPage.profilesSubtitle: Manage driver profiles and their connected devices.
profileManagementPage.provisioningDevice:
Provisioning device, issuing tokens
and uploading to cloud.
profileManagementPage.qrHintOffline:
Scan this QR with your phone to pair.
(offline mode — full data in QR)
profileManagementPage.qrHintOnline:
Scan this QR with your phone to pair.
Code expires in 10 minutes.
profileManagementPage.removeDeviceConten: This will unassign the device from the profile.
profileManagementPage.removeProfileConte: This will remove the profile and all assigned devices.
profileManagementPage.subscriptionRequir: Subscription required to add new devices
profileManagementPage.tokenIssued: Token issued. Scan QR to provision:
profileManagementPage.wrongKindMessage: That's a {detected}, not a {expected}. Tap a {expected} on the reader.
raceModePage.holdBrakeHint: Hold the brake, hit START, then floor it on GO
remoteDebugSettings.activeSessionInfo: Your support agent can see and run commands on this device. Tap End to disconnect them at any time.
remoteDebugSettings.description: Lets Pulse support open a terminal on this device — only with your active consent. They give you a 6-digit PIN; you enter it below. The session ends automatically after 15 minutes (or 5 minutes idle).
remoteDebugSettings.endDialogContent: Your support agent will lose access immediately.
remoteDebugSettings.enterPinError: Enter the PIN from your support agent.
remoteDebugSettings.enterPinSubtext: Your support agent will give you a 6-digit code.
remoteDebugSettings.pinAccepted: PIN accepted — waiting for support agent
remoteDebugSettings.waitingSessionInfo: When your support agent opens the terminal in their dashboard, the session will become Active here.
settings.title: Settings
settingsPage.advancedDescription: Niche tools and developer-facing settings. Most owners never need to open anything here.
settingsPage.hdmiParkOnly: HDMI Input is only available when the car is in Park.
settingsPage.title: Settings
shortcutsCard.title: Shortcuts
softwareUpdateSettings.raceModeDialogCon:
Paperwork's in. ✅ The stewards are watching now.

But a license isn't handed out this easy — go prove you belong on the grid. You'll know it when you find it.
softwareUpdateSettings.title: Software Update
softwareUpdateSettings.upToDate: Your app is up to date
statsStrip.editHint: Drag to reorder · tap ⇄ to replace
termsPrivacyPage.disagreeDialogContent: You must accept the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy to use Pulse. If you do not agree, you will not be able to use the app.
termsPrivacyPage.scrollHintBoth: Scroll to the bottom of both documents to continue
termsPrivacyPage.scrollHintPrivacy: Scroll to the bottom of Privacy Policy
termsPrivacyPage.scrollHintTerms: Scroll to the bottom of Terms of Service
termsPrivacyPage.subtitle: Please read and scroll through both documents below to continue.
termsPrivacyPage.title: Pulse Legal Agreement
themeSettings.strategyAutoByTime: By time of day — local sunrise / sunset
themeSettings.strategyManual: Off — use the mode I picked above
themeSettings.subtitle: Pick a base mode and an accent color. Changes apply instantly.
themeSettings.title: Theme
thirdPartyServices.apiKeyInfo: API key is provided in app configuration and will be used when sending telemetry.
tripMapView.gpsUnavailable: GPS was unavailable for this trip.
tripsHistory.deleteTripContent: Remove this trip from the log? This does not affect Trip A/B/Total counters.
tripsHistory.noTripsSubtitle: Trips are saved automatically when the car turns off after driving.
tripsHistory.subtitle: Every trip is saved automatically when you turn the car off. Tap a row to see the full breakdown.
tripsRangeSettings.carEstimatedRangeSubt: Uses the vehicle computer's range estimate
tripsRangeSettings.infoText: Profile range is calculated from your driving efficiency history multiplied by the current battery capacity. It adapts to your personal driving style over time. Resetting clears all accumulated efficiency data and starts fresh.
tripsRangeSettings.profileEfficiencySubt: Rolling efficiency data for the current profile.
tripsRangeSettings.rangeSourceSubtitle: Choose which range estimate to display in the header.
tripsRangeSettings.resetDialogContent: This will clear the rolling efficiency data for the current profile. The range estimate will show the car's CAN range until new data is collected.
tripsRangeSettings.title: Trips & Range
vehicleControlCard.title: Vehicle Control
vehicleSettings.androidAutoCarPlaySubtit: Automatically turn off the Android Auto / CarPlay dongle when the the car is off, and turn it back on when the car is on. This saves power and prevents your phone from connecting to Pulse when it is off
vehicleSettings.autoReturnToPulseInfo: Will auto-return to Pulse when you finish reversing within 1 minute
vehicleSettings.autoReturnToPulseSubtitl: The car will automatically switch back to Pulse if you finish reversing within a minute.
vehicleSettings.autoSwitchOnBrakePressSu: When the brake pedal is pressed to wake the car, automatically switch the screen from ICC to Pulse after a short delay. Disable to keep whatever screen was last shown.
vehicleSettings.autoSwitchOnBrakePressTi: Auto-switch to Pulse on brake press
vehicleSettings.bleKeyReadError: Failed to trigger BLE key reading: {v}
vehicleSettings.bleKeyTimeoutError: Timeout: Could not retrieve BLE key from vehicle
vehicleSettings.carlinkit5GhzConfigSaved: Config saved — please power-cycle the dongle manually for it to take effect
vehicleSettings.carlinkit5GhzSwitchSucce: Switched to 5 GHz Ch 149 — dongle restarted
vehicleSettings.carlinkit5GhzSwitchingIn: Switching CarPlay/AA WiFi to 5 GHz Ch 149 (≤10 s)…
vehicleSettings.carlinkitAutoOffEnabled: Android Auto / CarPlay will turn off when ignition is off
vehicleSettings.carlinkitRestartFailedOf: Restart failed: could not turn dongle off (root/sysfs?)
vehicleSettings.carlinkitRestartFailedOn: Dongle was turned off but did not come back on — try unplug/replug
vehicleSettings.clockOverrideEnabled: Clock override enabled — broadcasting Pulse time
vehicleSettings.currentTripSourceSubtitl: Choose how the "Current Trip" tile and the instant energy graph are calculated. Trip A, Trip B and Total are always Pulse-calculated.
vehicleSettings.donglePowerCycleInfo: The dongle will be powered off when ignition turns off, and powered on when ignition turns on
vehicleSettings.pkcAutoConnectEnabled: PKC will now auto-connect on app startup
vehicleSettings.pkcAutoConnectSubtitle: Automatically connect Pulse to PKC on app startup using the stored secured key.
vehicleSettings.pkcManagementSubtitle: Configure Pulse with PKC (PKC20 or PKC21.X).
vehicleSettings.pkcWillAutoConnectOnStar: PKC will connect automatically when the app starts
vehicleSettings.pulseAutoSwitchDisabled: Pulse will no longer auto-switch on brake press
vehicleSettings.pulseCalculationOptionSu: Same math as Trip A/B/Total — battery capacity delta over odometer delta for the current ignition session.
vehicleSettings.pulseWillTakeOverAfterDe: Pulse will take over the screen {v}s after the brake pedal is pressed
vehicleSettings.pulseWillTakeOverImmedia: Pulse will take over the screen immediately when the brake pedal is pressed
vehicleSettings.switchToPulseOnBrakePres: Switch to Pulse on brake press
vehicleSettings.timezoneSetFailed: Failed to set timezone (will retry on next override start)
vehicleSettings.title: Vehicle
vehicleSettings.turnOffWhenCarIsOff: Turn off when car is off
vehicleSettings.unitsSubtitle: Choose display units for distance and temperature.
vehicleSettings.vcuDataOptionSubtitle: Uses the car's reported trip distance, energy and efficiency.
welcomePage.addMoreProfilesHint: You can add more profiles later in Settings
welcomePage.pleaseEnterFirstAndLastName: Please enter first and last name
welcomePage.subtitle:
Thanks for choosing Pulse — your friendly EV companion.
Let's set up your first profile so you can get personalized vehicle features.
welcomePage.title: Welcome to Pulse
